title: prunella vulgaris subsp. asiatica
intro: Prunella vulgaris subsp. asiatica, commonly known as Asian self-heal, is a subspecies of the self-heal plant, a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Prunella vulgaris subsp. asiatica include its ability to boost the immune system, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health concerns. Herbal preparations of Asian self-heal can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
